releases.title: collectZ v3.4.151
releases.summary: This patch cleans up the Plex admin operating model after the main Plex integration run. The settings surface now explains which Plex actions are manual, which can run on schedules, which are webhook-triggered hints, and which writeback actions stay explicit user controls.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Adds a Plex operating-model readback panel to Integrations.
Renames user-facing reconciliation scan controls from preview wording to read-only check wording.
Clarifies that manual and scheduled Plex sync share the same safe create/update policy and send ambiguous rows to conflict review.
Clarifies that Plex webhooks can feed import hints while watched-state and rating writeback remain manual controls.
Moves the Plex operating-model cleanup task out of backlog and into the completed roadmap milestone.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.150
releases.summary: This patch moves Plex section discovery onto the provider-advertised sections root at runtime. When Plex advertises `/library/sections/all` through the official library provider, collectZ uses it; when it is absent or unavailable, collectZ keeps the existing `/library/sections` fallback.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Updates Plex section discovery to resolve the sections root from `/media/providers`.
Uses `/library/sections/all` when the official Plex library provider advertises it.
Falls back to `/library/sections` if provider discovery fails, the root is not advertised, or the advertised root is unavailable.
Adds a fake-PMS smoke proof covering advertised-root success, advertised-root failure fallback, and non-advertised fallback.
Keeps Plex item import, reconciliation, webhooks, watched-state writeback, rating writeback, and automatic sync behavior otherwise unchanged.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.149
releases.summary: This patch corrects the Plex provider import migration contract. `/media/providers` is now treated as capability discovery, while future import migration resolves the official Plex library provider's advertised documented `/library/...` paths before any import behavior changes.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.148
releases.summary: This patch polishes the Plex Now Playing display for homes with more than one active Plex session. The main display still focuses on the first active session, and can now show a compact readback of other active sessions when that display preference is enabled.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.147
releases.summary: This patch adds a read-only real Plex Media Server provider item-row parity proof. It uses saved Plex settings, probes provider-advertised item listing candidates with small page limits, and records whether those rows carry enough metadata to consider a future import migration.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.146
releases.summary: This patch adds a read-only Plex provider item-listing discovery proof. It checks whether `/media/providers` can expose safe content listing candidates before any future migration away from the current legacy section import path.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.145
releases.summary: This patch adds a read-only Plex provider/API import parity proof before any future migration away from the current library-section import path.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.144
releases.summary: This patch adds a guarded attach-existing path for Plex reconciliation conflicts so an admin can link a Plex identity to an existing collectZ title when the identifiers are safe.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.143
releases.summary: This patch adds a durable Plex reconciliation conflict review queue so unsafe Plex sync matches can be resolved intentionally after automated sync.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.142
releases.summary: This patch makes Plex TV watched-state writeback episode-aware instead of blocking TV series outright.
